name: Aviation & Drones
intro: Commercial aviation, general aviation and the fast-growing drone sector all depend on accurate atmospheric, weather and land information. Copernicus services support safe routing, environmental compliance, U-Space corridor planning and emergency response across the airspace.
eoRole: Earth observation provides the atmospheric, weather and land-surface information that aviation and drone operations depend on — from volcanic-ash and air-quality hazard detection to obstacle and terrain awareness. It complements navigation and surveillance data to improve safety and efficiency across the airspace, at scales and update rates that ground sensors alone cannot match.

needs.text: Route aircraft safely around volcanic ash and high-aerosol hazards
Plan urban drone delivery corridors that avoid poor air quality
Design safe low-altitude U-Space corridors and ground-risk zones
Make airport infrastructure resilient to a changing climate
Provide current hazard maps for emergency-response UAV missions

copernicusRole.description: Atmospheric composition forecasts — volcanic ash, aerosols, ozone, and near-surface air quality used in aviation meteorology and drone corridor planning.
Urban Atlas and High Resolution Layers underpin U-Space ground-risk mapping and obstacle-aware route generation.
ERA5 reanalysis and projections support airport climate resilience and long-term planning.
Rapid mapping activations provide situational awareness for emergency UAV deployment and air-traffic disruption.

name: Rail
intro: Europe's rail network spans over 200,000 km of track across diverse climates and geologies. Infrastructure managers use Copernicus to anticipate ground movement, climate hazards, vegetation encroachment and thermal stress — improving safety and timetable resilience.
eoRole: Earth observation gives rail infrastructure managers a synoptic, repeatable view of the entire network and its surroundings. It reveals slow ground movement, vegetation growth and climate hazards that are impractical to monitor comprehensively from the ground, allowing maintenance to shift from reactive inspection to data-driven prioritisation.

needs.text: Prevent heat-induced track buckling during extreme temperatures
Detect slow embankment and cutting movement before defects appear
Monitor vegetation encroachment near tracks and overhead lines
Adjust asset-renewal design standards for future climate
Manage the network during flood events

copernicusRole.description: Millimetre-precision ground motion data lets infrastructure managers prioritise inspection by deformation hotspot.
Climate reanalysis and projections feed rail temperature forecasting and long-term adaptation planning.
Land cover and vegetation products support corridor monitoring and encroachment detection.
Rapid mapping for flood and landslide events affecting rail operations.

name: Road & Automotive
intro: Road networks are Europe's most extensive transport infrastructure, while the automotive sector is transforming through ADAS, autonomous driving and electrification. Copernicus supports network risk management, climate-resilient planning, HD-map validation and air-quality compliance.
eoRole: Earth observation delivers wide-area, frequently-updated information on terrain, land cover, weather and hazards across vast road networks. It underpins network risk management and resilience planning, and provides the up-to-date ground-truth that high-definition mapping for assisted and automated driving requires.

needs.text: Make network closure decisions during floods
Monitor slope and pavement stability along road corridors
Keep HD maps for ADAS and automated driving up to date
Site and specify EV charging infrastructure for thermal extremes
Support low-emission zones and in-vehicle air-quality features

copernicusRole.description: Flood and wildfire rapid mapping supports real-time network management and emergency response.
Ground motion time series reveal slow subsidence under roads, enabling proactive maintenance.
Imperviousness, land cover and Urban Atlas inform HD-map validation, drainage risk and planning.
Climate projections support road asset risk screening and EV infrastructure planning.
Roadside air quality forecasts feed in-vehicle exposure models and low-emission-zone tools.

name: Marine, Maritime & Inland Waterways
intro: Shipping, ports, fisheries and inland-waterway operators depend on accurate ocean, sea-ice, water-level and weather information. Copernicus marine and land services support route optimisation, safe navigation, water-quality monitoring and coastal-infrastructure resilience.
eoRole: Earth observation is uniquely suited to the marine domain, offering all-weather, wide-area monitoring of the ocean surface, sea ice, water quality and vessel activity far beyond the reach of in-situ sensors. It turns vast, data-sparse waters into a continuously observed operating environment for navigation, surveillance and environmental protection.

needs.text: Optimise ship routes to cut fuel use and emissions
Navigate safely through sea ice
Plan inland-waterway navigability and barge loading
Get early warning of algal blooms and water-quality issues
Detect vessels and oil spills for maritime surveillance

copernicusRole.description: Ocean currents, waves, sea level, sea ice and biogeochemistry products for navigation, route optimisation and water-quality monitoring.
Flood awareness and rapid mapping for inland waterways and coastal events; EFAS/GloFAS discharge forecasts.
Water and wetness, coastal-zone and land-cover products for inland-waterway and coastal monitoring.
Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2/3 optical imagery for vessel detection, oil-spill monitoring and water turbidity.

name: Urban Development
intro: Cities consume most of Europe's energy and resources while occupying a fraction of its surface. Urban planners and city authorities use Copernicus to monitor urban growth, heat, green space, building energy performance and flood risk — supporting climate-resilient, liveable cities.
eoRole: Earth observation provides consistent, comparable, repeatable measurements of the urban environment — heat, green space, soil sealing, subsidence and air quality — across whole cities and over time. It enables planners to base decisions on evidence rather than assumption, and to benchmark progress against climate and liveability targets.

needs.text: Identify and cool urban heat islands
Prioritise building energy renovation
Monitor green space and soil sealing
Assess and reduce urban flood risk
Manage urban public space dynamically

copernicusRole.description: Urban Atlas, imperviousness, tree-cover and land-surface-temperature products are the backbone of urban monitoring and digital twins.
Urban air-quality and emissions data help prioritise building renovation and monitor residential emissions.
Climate projections and extreme-event indices support urban climate adaptation strategies.
Flood mapping and risk products underpin urban flood-resilience analysis.
